topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to energy production, environmental protection, and the promotion of renewable energy technologies.

Document 1: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act
prohibit a person selling such fuel who complies with such regulations from being liable
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act
include hydrogen sulfide in the list of hazardous air pollutants
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
tax credit for producing electricity from an Indian coal production facility
Document 4: Electric Drive Vehicle Deployment Act of 2010
financial assistance to states for the deployment of electric drive vehicles
Document 5: Marine and Hydrokinetic Renewable Energy Promotion Act of 2011
require the program of marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy technology research
Document 6: Consumer-First Energy Act of 2008
deny major integrated oil companies a tax deduction for income
Document 7: Coal Liquid Fuel Act
authorize the Secretary of Energy to enter into standby loan agreements
Document 8: Phantom Fuels Elimination Act of 2014
revise the renewable fuel program by requiring advanced biofuel
Document 9: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978
require a retail electric supplier to submit to the Secretary of Energy
Document 10: Securing America's Future with Energy and Sustainable Technologies Act
revise the renewable fuel program, including by revising the meaning of 'advanced biofuel'

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the planning, construction, and management of water resources, including water recycling, conservation, and infrastructure improvements.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the Magna Water District, Utah, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities needed to establish recycled water distribution
Limits the federal share of the capital cost of the project to 25% of the total cost
Document 2: direct the Secretary of the Army to assess the total operation and maintenance needs of all harbors and inland harbors
submit to specified congressional committees a report on commercial navigation projects for such harbors
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Commerce to award a grant to a state or local government of a coastal state, a nonprofit organization, or a fishing cooperative for projects to acquire real property in a coastal state to provide access to commercial fishermen
make improvements to real property owned by an eligible entity in a coastal state
Document 4: amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to repeal a provision that requires any fishery management plan
establish catch limits for the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery
Document 5: directs the Chief of Engineers to complete a study to determine the feasibility of constructing a multipurpose water resources project
provide more reliable flood control and better sediment control
Document 6: authorize the Department of the Interior to provide cost-shared financial assistance and other long-term agreements to nonfederal participants
projects for the reclamation and reuse of municipal, industrial, domestic, and agricultural wastewater
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the Rancho California Water District, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities for water recycling
distribution of non-potable water supplies in Southern Riverside County, California
Document 8: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the Olivenhain Municipal Water District, California, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of projects to treat, reclaim, and reuse impaired surface water
Limits the federal share to 25% of the total project costs
Document 9: establish a Rare Cats and Canids Conservation Fund as a separate account within the Multinational Species Conservation Fund
provide assistance for the conservation of rare felids and rare canids
Document 10: establish aquatic invasive species reduction and prevention requirements for vessels
promulgate ballast water management regulations and certification procedures

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and policies related to Medicare, focusing on healthcare services, coverage, and payment mechanisms.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to begin the process to develop and implement a prior authorization process for certain durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, and supplies.
certain durable medical equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, and supplies.
Document 2: Medicare Orthotics and Prosthetics Improvement Act of 2015
Decisions about Medicare coverage of orthotics and prosthetics on or after January 1, 2016, must take into account the complexity of the item as well as supplier qualifications.

topic.name: Consumer and Civil Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at protecting consumer rights, civil liberties, and specific groups such as minors and military personnel.

Document 1: civil relief for violations of the protections on consumer credit extended to members of the Armed Forces and their dependents
Document 2: Amends the Hobbs Act to authorize imposition of a fine of up to $100,000, 20 years' imprisonment, or both for obstructing commerce by robbery or extortion
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to award grants to enable states to invest in practices and technology designed to expedite voting
Document 4: Amends the Black Lung Benefits Act, with respect to claims filed on or after the effective date of the Black Lung Benefits Amendments of 1981
Document 5: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Document 6: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to define 'oppressive child labor'
Document 7: Prohibits any person or organization from performing an abortion on an unemancipated minor unless written notification is provided to each parent
Document 8: Requires each employer of a law enforcement officer to pay for any liability arising from conduct in the course of official duty
Document 9: Amends the Defense of Marriage Act to repeal a provision that prohibited a state from recognizing same sex marriage
Document 10: Prohibits U.S. courts from recognizing rights by an individual of a mark that was used in connection with a business confiscated by the Cuban government

topic.name: Social Services Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving social services and support systems for vulnerable populations including firefighters, disabled individuals, children in foster care, and families.

Document 1: Firefighter Safety Enhancement Act of 2010
authorize the use of firefighter assistance grant funds to repair, construct, and reconstruct fire stations
Document 2: Reauthorizes Indian Health Service (IHS) programs
ensure quality health care for all tribal members
Document 3: prohibits states or local governments from denying community-based services
enables individuals to live in the community and lead an independent life
Document 4: require states to assist children in foster care in making the transition to independent living
authorize additional appropriations to enable states to take such additional steps
Document 5: permit the establishment of Job Corps centers in outlying areas
Document 6: exempt persons with disabilities from the prohibition against specified public housing rental assistance
Document 7: assess adult guardianship and conservatorship proceedings
implement changes deemed necessary as a result of the assessments
Document 8: provide wage replacement for eligible individuals responding to family caregiving needs
expand quality programs of early childhood home visitation
Document 9: provide legal representation for parents and legal guardians with respect to child welfare cases
increases funding for discretionary and targeted grants
Document 10: ensure compliance with U.S. treaty obligations under any multilateral child support convention
allows collection of past-due support from federal tax refunds

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative reforms and regulatory changes across different sectors including labor, immigration, bankruptcy, small business, and public health.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to revise requirements with respect to the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB)
Increases NLRB membership from five to six.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to permit an alien to be paroled into the United States or granted deferred action
on a case-by-case basis for urgent humanitarian reasons or significant public benefit.
Document 3: Requires each federal department and agency to report to the congressional appropriations committees an operating budget that reflects a 5% reduction
from the amount requested for it in the President's most recent budget submission.
Document 4: Requires a state to return any unobligated Community Oriented Policing Services Program (COPS) funds
until it has in effect laws and policies that prohibit the issuance of a license to such an alien.
Document 5: This bill amends the federal judicial code to convert certain temporary bankruptcy judges to permanent bankruptcy judges
and authorize the appointment of additional bankruptcy judges in Delaware and Michigan.
Document 6: Allows a small business operating in the United States to elect to be exempt from any federal rule or regulation
issued on or after January 20, 2009.
Document 7: Prohibits a federal agency from proposing or finalizing any midnight rule that would likely result in an annual effect on the economy of $100 million or more
or significant adverse effects on competition, employment, investment, productivity, innovation.
Document 8: This bill amends the Congressional Review Act to allow Congress to consider a joint resolution to disapprove multiple regulations
that federal agencies have submitted for congressional review.
Document 9: This bill amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require a state to certify that it has established
administrative procedures regarding nonmedical exemptions from state immunization requirements.
Document 10: The U.S. International Trade Commission shall conduct a process for the submission and consideration of petitions for duty suspensions and reductions
and report to Congress on the effects of duty suspensions and reductions enacted pursuant to this Act.

topic.name: Consumer Protection and Information Transparency
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at protecting consumers' rights and ensuring transparency in communications and information services.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal entity from compelling a covered person to testify or produce any document related to information obtained or created as part of engaging in journalism
Defines 'journalism' as the gathering, preparing, collecting, photographing, recording, writing, editing, reporting, or publishing of news or information.
Document 2: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring a commercial mobile service provider to notify a subscriber who has used in excess of a certain percentage of the monthly limit
Ensures that any notice of possible excess usage is not charged to the subscriber's account.
Document 3: Requires an individual or entity that operates a website that provides access to international travel services to provide information regarding the health and safety risks of overseas vacation destinations
Treats a violation as an unfair or deceptive act or practice under the Federal Trade Commission Act.
Document 5: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring providers and distributors of prepaid calling cards to accurately disclose applicable terms and conditions
Requires such disclosures to include the provider's name, customer service telephone number, and hours of service.
Document 6: Requires the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to provide a substantive written response to any written correspondence from a taxpayer not later than 30 days
To provide the taxpayer a written notification describing the information disclosed to any governmental entity.
Document 7: Allows multichannel video programming distributors to provide subscribers with any channel of video programming on an a la carte basis
Defines 'a la carte' as offering video programming for wholesale or retail purchase on an individual, per-channel basis.
Document 8: Repeals provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 concerning the carriage of distant television stations
Directs the FCC to repeal related FCC rules and eliminate restrictions on the number of...
Document 9: Prohibits federal funding to organizations incorporated for specified purposes related to broadcasting and programming over noncommercial educational radio broadcast stations
Revises the Public Broadcasting Fund allocation formula.
Document 10: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require telecommunication carriers to provide call location information of users to law enforcement agencies
Directs the Attorney General to establish a program to provide education and training to state and local law enforcement agencies.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and overseeing financial institutions, mortgage practices, and consumer protections in the financial sector.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to require specified mortgagees to indemnify HUD for payment of a mortgage insurance claim if the mortgage was not materially originated or underwritten in accordance with HUD requirements.
Establish a program to review the cause of each early term default and claim on such mortgages.
Document 2: Repeals the subpoena and enforcement powers of the Director of the Federal Insurance Office in the Department of the Treasury.
Prescribes the treatment of data collected by financial regulators from a nonbank financial company that is an insurance company.
Document 3: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 (RESPA) to state that no prohibited kickback or unearned fee incident to a real estate settlement service involving a federally related mortgage loan shall be deemed to include homeowner warranties.
Requires any person who has contracted to receive payment from a provider of such services for recommending the purchase of a home warranty to give the potential purchaser a written notice.
Document 4: Prohibit any financial institution that receives assistance under the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) from charging its customers more than 18% interest on credit card accounts.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) and the Federal Credit Union Act (FCUA) to redefine the standard maximum deposit insurance amount and the standard maximum share insurance amount as being $250,000.
Authorize the FDIC to impose special assessments upon depository institution holding companies to recover losses to the DIF.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act of 1978 to require a federal financial institutions regulatory agency to make a final examination report to a financial institution within 60 days.
Grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination contained in a final report of examination.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require creditors to report annually to the Federal Reserve Board on the terms and conditions of all business, marketing, and promotional agreements and college affinity card agreements.
Directs the Comptroller General to review such reports and creditor marketing practices periodically.
Document 8: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to cover energy transactions and extends the regulatory authority of the Commodities Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).
Prohibits the CFTC from exempting energy transactions from futures trading regulation.
Document 9: Provides immunity from liability for a supervisor, compliance officer, or registered representative for disclosing suspected exploitation of a senior citizen.
A covered financial institution may provide such training to each of its supervisors, compliance officers, or registered representatives.
Document 10: Declares it is US policy that the principal economic responsibilities of the Government are to establish both long-term economic growth and increases in living standards.
Revises the mandate of the Board and the Federal Open Market Committee to require them to establish a numerical definition of the term 'price stability'.

topic.name: Water and Environmental Health Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation related to water management, environmental health, and commemorative initiatives addressing historical events and their impacts on communities.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to direct the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) to establish the Office of Water
place primary emphasis on providing safe drinking water, sanitation, and hygiene.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish an interagency working group to discuss environmental health concerns

establish a program to provide funding and technical assistance to state or local governments affected or potentially affected by an activity or proposed activity to prepare health impact assessments.

topic.name: Agricultural and Food Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agriculture, food assistance, and dietary regulations in the United States.

Document 2: Kosher and Halal Food Act of 2013
modify the labeling of the commodities list under the emergency food assistance program
Document 3: Community-Supported Agriculture Promotion Act
promote community-supported agriculture
Document 4: Dairy Pricing Deregulation Act
revise the terms of federal milk marketing orders
Document 6: Assuring You Uniform Dietary Assistance (AYUDA) Act
provide the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands with the same food assistance benefits
Document 7: Emergency Farm Assistance Act
provide assistance to producers of specialty crops in disaster counties
Document 8: School Food Fresh Act
establish a task force to coordinate commodity programs

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing benefits and support for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Amends titles II and XVI of the Social Security Act to treat as a disability for OASDI and SSI disability payment purposes any service-connected total disability.
Applies the same treatment to related child's insurance and widow's and widower's insurance benefits.
Document 2: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation.
Concerns service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war.
Document 3: Increases the maximum monthly rates of hazardous duty pay and hostile fire pay.
Includes provisions for combat-related injury rehabilitation pay.
Document 4: Allows former members of the Armed Forces to transfer educational assistance to their dependents.
Applies to service between September 11, 2001, and September 30, 2011.
Document 5: Makes permanent the authority for the provision of government markers or memorial headstones for veterans buried in private cemeteries.
Amends the Veterans Education and Benefits Expansion Act of 2001.
Document 6: Requires military personnel who give birth to be given 42 days of convalescent leave.
Allows additional leave but makes them ineligible for basic pay during that time.
Document 8: Revises the amount payable to veterans for education under the post-9/11 veterans' educational assistance program.
Provides a monthly housing stipend for distance learning.
Document 9: Deems service of a person as a member of the United States Cadet Nurse Corps as active military service for VA purposes.
Requires the Secretary of Defense to issue an honorable discharge within one year.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish the Military Valor Roll of Honor registry.
Shall contain names and citations of members awarded medals for valor.

topic.name: Public Health Legislative Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Children's Hospital GME Support Reauthorization Act of 2013 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to extend and reauthorize appropriations for payments to children's hospitals for expenses associated with operating approved graduate medical residency training programs.
Document 2: Independent Drug Education and Outreach Act of 2008 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services, acting through the Director of the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, to award grants or contracts for educational materials concerning prescription drugs.
Document 3: Violence Against Women Health Initiative Act of 2011 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize and revise a program to award grants to strengthen the response of health care systems to domestic violence.
Document 4: Zika Is Preventable Plan Act or the ZIPP Act - This bill authorizes funds for Ebola response to also be used for Zika virus response.
Document 5: Co-Prescribing Saves Lives Act of 2016 - This bill requires training guidelines for health care providers on prescribing pain medications and opioid overdose reversal drugs.
Document 6: Veterans E-Health & Telemedicine Support Act of 2012 - Allows health care professionals to provide treatment through telemedicine regardless of location.
Document 7: Suicide Prevention Research INnovaTion Act or the SPRINT Act - Requires research on determinants of self-directed and other violence associated with mental illness.
Document 8: Healthy Workforce Act of 2007 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers a tax credit for providing wellness programs.
Document 9: Early Hearing Detection and Intervention Act of 2010 - Expands the newborns and infants hearing loss program to include diagnostic services.
Document 10: School Protection Act of 2009 - Authorizes grants to train school nurses as first responders in case of biological or chemical attacks.

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational systems, facilities, and opportunities for diverse student populations.

Document 1: expands the programs eligible to participate in federal student aid
leads to an industry-recognized credential
Document 2: establish an in-school facility innovation program contest
create or expand teacher and administrator pipelines for teachers and administrators of Native American students
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Education to make grants to states for the modernization, renovation, or repair of public schools
to make them safe, healthy, high-performing, and technologically up-to-date
Document 4: award competitive grants to up to 10 states for pilot programs to determine the effectiveness of assessing disabled students
provide quality instruction to such students
Document 5: allocate funds to states for competitive matching grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) for school repair, renovation, and construction
consider the percentage of poor children each LEA serves
Document 6: revise requirements for statewide and local workforce investment systems to include adult education activities
assist adults with limited English proficiency to improve their English and mathematical skills
Document 7: authorize local educational agencies (LEAs) to use school improvement funds to transform schools into community schools
address students' academic, health, and developmental needs
Document 8: direct the Secretary of Agriculture to deliver commodity assistance to states for their school breakfast programs
Document 9: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to states to provide scholarships for preschool teachers
increase the compensation of teachers who are making progress toward a degree in early childhood education
Document 10: require each institution of higher education (IHE) to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment
authorize the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to IHEs to prevent harassment

topic.name: Land Conveyance and Recognition Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts focused on land conveyance, federal recognition of tribes, and the management of natural resources.

Document 1: Designates certain land in the Mount Baker-Snoqualmie National Forest as wilderness.
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate the Pratt River as a wild river.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for services and benefits provided by the United States.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to convey a parcel of real property to the State of Tennessee.
Requires the State to use such real property as a public firing range.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey parcels of National Forest System land in Box Elder County, Utah.
Requires the town to use the land conveyed for public purposes.
Document 5: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to set forth land selection rights of the 13th Regional Corporation.
Authorizes the Corporation to select up to 1,453,388 acres from specified public lands.
Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Directs the Secretary to acquire trust title to 200 acres of land for the Tribe.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal land to the state of Utah in exchange for non-federal land.
Sets forth provisions regarding land administration and easements.
Document 8: Ratifies the Salmon Lake Area Land Ownership and Consolidation Agreement.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take actions under the Agreement.
Document 9: Declares portions of the Delaware River to be nonnavigable waters of the United States.
The Army Corps of Engineers shall make public interest determinations for proposed projects.
Document 10: Requires the conveyance of specified National Forest System land to the Indian Nations Council, Inc.
States that access to the conveyed land shall be from the adjacent land of the Council.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing financial assistance and support for small businesses in various contexts, including disaster recovery, rural housing, and veteran-owned enterprises.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to obtaining the best available collateral for a disaster loan
Prohibits the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) from requiring the small business owner to use the owner's primary residence as collateral
Document 2: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing
States that any area classified as 'rural' shall continue to be so classified until the receipt of data from the decennial census in the year 2020
Document 3: Reinstates the authority for Small Business Administration (SBA) low-interest refinancing of small business debt
Repeals a provision of the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 which terminated such authority
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to extend a temporary increase from $350,000 to $1 million in the maximum loan amount under the Express Loan Program
Document 5: Directs each federal agency that provides contracts, grants, loans, or loan guarantees to businesses to require information on the number of employees employed in the United States
Prohibits subsequent federal assistance to businesses that lay off a greater percentage of workers in the United States than in other countries
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to authorize the Small Business Administration (SBA) to guarantee the payment of up to $4 billion per fiscal year
Establishes an early-stage investment program to provide equity financing to support early-stage businesses
Document 7: Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make loans directly to small businesses owned and controlled by veterans
Directs the Administrator to reduce any applicable requirement relating to the veteran's credit score
Document 8: Requires at least 10% of funds for high-speed rail projects to be expended through small business concerns owned by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals
Encourages participation by veteran-owned small businesses in federal contracting opportunities
Document 9: Amends the Small Business Act to allow the head of an executive agency to consider only a small business verified in the small business database maintained by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
Requires prime contractors to consider only verified small business concerns in awarding subcontracts
Document 10: Amends the Export Enhancement Act of 1988 to require the Secretary of Commerce to provide small and medium-sized businesses with export licensing compliance assistance
Establishes an Interagency Task Force on Export Control Assistance and Relief for Small and Medium-Sized Businesses

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and Border Security
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and initiatives related to immigration policies, border security, and biometric identification in the United States.

Document 1: conduct in the maritime environment a program for the mobile biometric identification of suspected individuals
enhance border security
Document 2: establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa for a sponsored alien entrepreneur
generate required levels of employment, revenue, or capital investment
Document 3: make up to 55,000 visas available in FY2016 and subsequent fiscal years to qualified immigrants
have a doctorate degree in a field of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics
Document 4: assess the needs of Central American countries to maintain the security of their international borders
work with Canada, Mexico, and the countries of Central America
Document 5: make the E-Verify Program permanent and mandatory
Document 6: direct the Department of Homeland Security to complete the required 700-mile southwest border fencing
achieve operational control over U.S. international land and maritime borders
Document 7: eliminate the per country numerical limitation for employment-based immigrants
increase the per country numerical limitation for family based immigrants
Document 8: an alien who fails to appear at a removal proceeding shall be fined and imprisoned
Document 9: prohibits the admission of K-1 nonimmigrants and the issuance of K-1 visas
Document 10: restricts the forms of individual identification that may be accepted by the federal government

topic.name: Crime Prevention and Law Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at preventing crime, enhancing law enforcement, and improving justice system processes.

Document 1: Authorize grants to combat gang activity
Expand programs for safe and drug-free schools
Document 2: Increase prison sentences for public officials convicted of crimes
Focus on bribery, fraud, extortion, or theft of public funds
Document 3: Amend laws regarding firearm possession and ownership
Prohibit regulations that discourage private ownership of firearms
Document 4: Develop a national strategy to reduce cybercrimes against individuals
Publish annual summary of cybercrime statistics
Document 5: Amend the Uniform Code of Military Justice for court-martial processes
Require a commissioned officer to determine trial proceedings
Document 6: Require notification of Congress for rewards under the rewards for justice program
Document 7: Amend definition of child exploitation to include illegal custody transfers
Document 8: Expand definition of armor piercing ammunition
Prohibit manufacture and sale of certain firearms
Document 9: Include criminal violations by aliens in the definition of aggravated felony
Document 10: Extend funding for sex offender management and registration programs
Grants to combat sexual abuse of children

topic.name: Federal Funding and Asset Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to federal funding, asset management, and financial assistance programs across various sectors.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to establish a program for the attachment of federal liens to public transportation assets.
Prohibits any asset transaction for an asset attached with a federal lien unless the lien is released.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for each fiscal year the amount determined under a specified formula for veterans health care programs.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to establish a program to provide funds to a state or local government, nonprofit firefighter association, or governmental or nongovernmental emergency medical services (EMS) organization.
Requires reports from recipients, and from the Secretary annually, to Congress.
Document 4: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2009 to increase the FY2010 amount for emergency food assistance program commodity purchases.
Document 5: Establishes a new process for the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to set annual fees for the issuance of special use permits for the use and occupancy of National Forest System land.
Requires the Secretary to report after 10 years on these annual fees in order to ensure that they reflect fair value for the use of System land.
Document 6: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to reduce the authorization for the Secretary of the Treasury to purchase troubled assets.
Corresponds to repayment by a financial institution of assistance provided under the Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP).
Document 7: Prohibits the use of federal funds to purchase or guarantee obligations of any state, municipal, local, or county government which has filed for bankruptcy.
Waives the application of this Act for federal assistance provided in response to a natural disaster.
Document 8: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to allow federal entities that utilize government station licenses to participate in the incentive auction program.
Establishes in the Treasury a Federal Spectrum Incentive Fund to be administered by the Office of Management and Budget.
Document 9: Approves the agreement and appendices signed by the United States and the Republic of Palau on September 3, 2010, in connection with the Compact of Free Association.
Extends funding for infrastructure maintenance and projects.
Document 10: Requires the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to continue rural mail delivery at its current level for the five-year period beginning on the enactment of this Act.
Limits the number of buy-outs offered to employees who work in rural post offices.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing government efficiency, transparency, and consumer protection across various sectors.

Document 1: Requires federal agencies to participate in a cooperative administrative support system to reduce duplication of services overseas.
Mandates a review of the system by the Comptroller General.
Document 2: Requires the FTC to publish an annual report on fraud targeting individuals aged 65 or older.
Mandates the FTC to outline its projected activities and enforcement actions related to elderly fraud.
Document 3: Instructs the SEC to require annual reports disclosing payments made to foreign governments for natural resources.
Ensures public access to this information through the SEC website.
Document 4: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to enhance consumer protections regarding credit reporting disputes.
Directs the CFPB to establish procedures for maximum accuracy in consumer reports.
Document 5: Mandates the use of plain language in health insurance documents to improve consumer understanding.
Requires guidance from the Secretary of Health and Human Services for implementation.
Document 6: Establishes a screening process for conflicts of interest in educational advisory committees.
Requires monitoring of technical assistance providers for compliance with conflict policies.
Document 7: Directs the EPA to develop guidelines for the remediation of former methamphetamine labs based on scientific knowledge.
Mandates collaboration with state and local governments in guideline adoption.
Document 8: Establishes a commission to develop accessibility guidelines for electronic instructional materials in higher education.
Provides a safe harbor from liability for institutions complying with these guidelines.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to utilize mail carriers as temporary enumerators for the census.
Limits the duties of carriers serving as enumerators to avoid conflicts.
Document 10: Establishes a Regulatory Improvement Commission to evaluate and recommend changes to outdated regulations.
Aims to reduce compliance costs and improve public safety through regulatory reform.

topic.name: U.S. Defense and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to U.S. defense, intelligence activities, and military support initiatives.

Document 1: Economic and Democratic Development Assistance for Afghanistan
assistance to women and girls
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) in awarding a contract for any major defense acquisition program
illegal subsidy has not been withdrawn
Document 3: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to the government of Israel
procurement, maintenance, and sustainment of the Iron Dome
Document 4: authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI)
Document 5: requires the Director of National Intelligence to study the extent to which contractors are used in the conduct of intelligence activities
to submit a report containing the results of the study
Document 6: assign at least one Sexual Assault Nurse Examiner-Adult/Adolescent to each brigade
appropriate Examiner training and certification
Document 7: Prohibits funds from being obligated or expended for military operations or activities within or above Iran's territory
requires the President within 24 hours after determining to use funds for such a military purpose to report
Document 8: expresses the sense of Congress that given the uncertain world
the planned drawdown of land forces should cease
Document 9: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to subject the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection to regular appropriations
subjects the Bureau to the regular authorization, budget, and appropriations process
Document 10: Prohibits either the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary of the Army from funding the transfer of personnel or functions from Fort Monmouth
until at least 30 days after the Comptroller General completes an audit

topic.name: Harmonized Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on temporary suspensions and exemptions of duties for specific goods and materials.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of formaldehyde polymers with aniline and with 4,4'-methylendianiline.
Document 2: This bill amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to exempt from duty treatment the residue of bulk cargo contained in instruments of international traffic previously exported from the United States.
Document 3: Congress finds... that the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States was amended on February 3, 2007, to provide that certain medium-density fiberboard shall include laminate boards bonded in whole or in part, or impregnated, with synthetic resins.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain fuel injection pumps.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing Fluopyram and Pyrimethanil.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on potassium persulfate encapsulated in cellulose acetate butyrate.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain single yarns of viscose rayon.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear covering the ankle.

topic.name: Animal Welfare and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving the welfare and safety of animals, particularly in breeding, research, and medical contexts.

Document 1: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'
Requires dealers to include on licensing applications the total number of dogs exempted from exercise
Document 4: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to make it unlawful to knowingly sponsor or exhibit an animal in dog fighting
Provides for imprisonment for up to five years for violations
Document 7: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to revise the list of permissible sources of dogs and cats used by research facilities
Monetary penalties are established for violations
Document 8: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug
Defines 'critical antimicrobial animal drug' as a drug intended for use in food-producing animals

topic.name: Pipeline Safety and Environmental Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety and environmental protections related to pipeline transportation and oil production.

Document 1: Prescribes or revises requirements for safety and environmental protection in pipeline transportation.
Subjects to a civil penalty of $250,000 per day any person that the Secretary of Transportation has found to have committed a major consequence violation.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to redefine the term 'pollutant' to exclude any deck runoff from a recreational vessel.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to require oil produced from federal leases in certain Arctic waters to be transported by pipeline to onshore facilities.
Document 4: Repeals the prohibition under the Consolidated Appropriations Act on the use of funds to prepare or publish final regulations regarding a commercial leasing program for oil shale resources.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to increase the number of Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration personnel to carry out the pipeline safety program.
Defines the term 'major consequence violation' to mean a violation that contributed to any incident resulting in environmental harm exceeding $250,000.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish an office to implement royalty and revenue management functions.
Document 7: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee with respect to federal onshore and offshore lands subject to a lease for production of oil or natural gas.

topic.name: Student Loan Repayment Options
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming and providing various repayment options for federal student loans.

Document 1: terminates existing federal student loan repayment plan options
creates a new version of the income-based repayment (IBR) plan
Document 2: allow recent college graduates to defer payment on their student loans
Limits that deferral period to a maximum of five years
Document 4: make the 3.4% interest rate on Direct Stafford loans applicable to loans disbursed between specific dates
Document 5: modifies federal student aid eligibility criteria
repeals a provision that suspends a student's federal student aid eligibility for drug offenses
Document 6: require institutions of higher education to include total aggregate amount of Direct Loans borrowed by the borrower
estimated number of payments and total amount to be paid to repay such a loan
Document 9: allow a borrower who is a victim of a terrorist attack to defer student loans
suspend repayments on student loans made under various federal programs
Document 10: require the Department of Education to annually give federal student loan borrowers detailed information
establish a process for annual verification of the borrower's annual income

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at altering tax provisions for estates, businesses, education, and individuals.

Document 1: Extends the estate and gift tax rates in effect in 2009
establish a permanent $3.5 million estate tax exclusion
Document 2: extend through 2016 the special depreciation allowance for business assets
increase the limitation on the election to accelerate the alternative minimum tax credit
Document 3: replace the Hope Scholarship and Lifetime Learning tax credits
increases the income threshold for reductions in the credit amount
Document 4: Prohibits a state from imposing a tax on the income earned in the state by nonresidents
Document 5: require plans to include certain part-time employees
determine non-discrimination compliance by considering only vested pension plan benefits
Document 6: allow a nonrefundable income tax credit of up to $500 for the purchase of a qualified hearing aid
Document 7: make permanent the 15-year recovery period classification for qualified leasehold improvement
Document 8: restore the unified credit against the estate and gift tax after 2009
establish the amount of such credit at $3.5 million
Document 9: make permanent provisions allowing a taxpayer to elect to deduct state and local general sales taxes
Document 10: exclude from gross income in 2009 and 2010 long-term capital gains
reduce corporate income tax rates in 2009 and 2010
